Tests object coercion in JavaScript by overriding `valueOf` and `toString` methods.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which are used to control how the object behaves in arithmetic and string operations. The `valueOf` method returns the number `1`, and the `toString` method returns the string representation `0`. The code includes an assertion that checks if the expression `object + 1` evaluates to `2`, demonstrating the use of the `valueOf` method in arithmetic operations. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on demonstrating how JavaScript handles type coercion and operator overloading through object methods.
